evals.prompt: Brainstorm 5 viral YouTube video concepts using a rubber duck as the central object or theme. For each concept, provide: a draft title, one-sentence premise, the wow factor moment, format, and a CTR score (1-5).
evals.expected_output: 5 distinct concepts using the 3-angle expansion framework (competition, scale, constraint). Each should have an extreme title, instantly-understandable premise, a specific wow-factor moment (something no other creator could do), identified format, and CTR score with rationale.
expectations: Produces exactly 5 distinct concepts (not minor variations of the same idea)
At least one concept uses a scale angle (world's largest, 10,000 rubber ducks, etc.)
At least one concept uses a competition or outcome-unknown angle
Each concept includes a specific wow factor moment (not generic)
Titles use extreme/superlative language and pass the 6-word simplicity test
CTR scores are differentiated (not all rated 5)

evals.prompt: A creator wants to do a video about teaching kids coding. The budget is $5,000. They say they need at least $50,000 to make it good. Apply the 'creativity saves money' principle to redesign the concept so it's more interesting AND costs under $5,000.
evals.expected_output: A redesigned concept that uses creative alternatives to money: a competition format, finding kids who already can code and having them face off, using free/donated prizes, finding a corporate sponsor, making the 'low budget' itself part of the hook. Should explicitly apply the principle that creativity is the answer, not budget.
expectations: Reframes the concept to be more creative rather than just more expensive
Provides at least one specific budget-saving creative alternative
The redesigned concept is MORE interesting than the original expensive version
Explicitly applies the 'creativity saves money' principle
New concept stays under or at $5,000
Draft title for the redesigned concept uses extreme/hook language

evals.prompt:
Evaluate these two video ideas using the 5-dimension idea rubric (CTR potential, simplicity, wow factor, emotional investment, format fit). Score each on all 5 dimensions and recommend which to produce:

Idea A: 'I trained for 1 year with an Olympic swimming coach and then competed against the coach'
Idea B: 'I built a swimming pool out of 50,000 water balloons'
evals.expected_output: Scores for both ideas on all 5 dimensions with reasoning. Idea B likely scores higher on CTR (visual uniqueness) and wow factor, while Idea A scores higher on emotional investment and format fit. The recommendation should clearly state which to produce and why, with suggestions for how to improve the weaker idea.
expectations: Scores both ideas on all 5 dimensions (CTR, simplicity, wow factor, emotional investment, format fit)
Scores are differentiated between the two ideas — not identical
Clear recommendation for which idea to produce with reasoning
Notes that Idea A has emotional investment but weak thumbnail/CTR potential
Notes that Idea B has strong CTR and wow factor but needs a stronger format/payoff
Suggests improvements for the weaker idea
